data.title: Rare earths developer Critical Metals jumps on relief over Trump's Greenland threats
data.description:
Critical Metals (CRML) +5.7% pre-market Thursday after President Trump said he would no longer impose his new Europe tariffs that were set to begin February 1 and announced a "framework" deal over Greenland.

Critical Metals (CRML), which is developing the Tanbreez rare earths project in Greenland, has seen its stock spike more than double since the beginning of the year and now has a market cap of $1.8B.

The company announced last week it signed a non-binding term sheet to form a 50-50 joint venture with Tariq Abdel Hadi Abdullah Al-Qahtani & Brothers Company to build a rare earth processing plant in Saudi Arabia for as much as $1.5B.

Critical Metals (CRML) said the term sheet outlines the framework for the development, financing, construction, and operation of a rare earth processing facility to create a fully integrated mine-to-processing supply chain, including long-term offtake rights for 25% of the Tanbreez rare earth concentrate production to Saudi Arabia.

The company disclosed Wednesday it has now entered into four non-binding term sheets for the offtake of rare earth concentrate extracted from Tanbreez.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Abbott Laboratories Q4 beats profit estimates, but misses on revenue; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
Rocket Lab updates on Neutron testing setback, first launch of 2026
data.description:
Rocket Lab Corporation (RKLB) announced that its Neutron rocket's Stage 1 tank ruptured during a hydrostatic pressure trial. The company noted that testing failures are not uncommon during qualification testing. "We intentionally test structures to their limits to validate structural integrity and safety margins to ensure the robust requirements for a successful launch can be comfortably met," updated Rocket Lab (RKLB).

Notably, there was no significant damage to the test structure or facilities, the next Stage 1 tank is already in production, and Neutron’s development campaign continues as the RKLB team reviews the Stage 1 test data, which will determine the extent of the impact to Neutron’s launch schedule.

Weighing in on the development, BTIG analyst Andre Madrid said the firm expects Neutron to complete one test launch and one paid launch in 2026, followed by four paid launches in 2027, seven in 2028, and nine in 2029. "However, depending on investigation results, initial launches could be pushed to the right," warned Madrid. The firm kept a Neutral rating on Rocket Lab (RKLB).

In a separate announcement, Rocket Lab (RKLB) said its first launch of 2026 was successful. The mission "The Cosmos Will See You Now" lifted off today from Rocket Lab Launch Complex 1 in Mahia, New Zealand. The mission successfully deployed two satellites into a 1,050 km circular Earth orbit for Open Cosmos, a first-time dedicated launch customer. The higher-altitude deployment showcased Electron's wide payload trajectory range, maintaining its reputation as a versatile and dependable small spacecraft launch vehicle that meets diverse mission requirements.

Shares of Rocket Lab (RKLB) were down 1.5% in premarket trading on Thursday after shedding 1.5% on Thursday as well.

data.title: Procter & Gamble in charts: FQ2 posts Y/Y gains across all segments except Family Care
MarketWise estimates Q4 billings to rise 42% Y/Y
data.description:
MarketWise (MKTW) on Thursday announced that Q4 billings, or net sales, are estimated at ~$79M, up 23% sequentially and 42% year-over-year.

Shares were +0.31% pre-market to $16.10.

For the full-year 2025, billings are estimated to come in at ~$271M, beating guidance of $250M.

Consolidated paid subscribers as of December 31, 2025, were 374K, while active free subscribers were 2.0M.

Cash and cash equivalents balance rose to $70M at December 31, 2025, from $51M at September 30, 2025.

data.title: McCormick expects rising costs in 2026 due to global trade dynamics; reports tepid results
data.description:
Shares in McCormick (MKC) are down in premarket trading after the spice and condiments manufacturing company guided a full-year 2026 profit forecast below expectations, reported weak fourth-quarter results, and warned that global trade dynamics continue to create headwinds, which would increase costs for the year.

MKC is down 6% at $62.56 before markets opened on Thursday.

The company, which produces Cholula and Frank's RedHot sauces, said it now expects adjusted EPS between $3.05 and $3.13, which is below the Bloomberg consensus estimate of $3.23. They also expect sales growth between 13% and 17% (mid. 15%; est. 14.63%) and operating income growth of 16% to 20% for 2026.

For 2026, the company sees foreign exchange rates favorably impacting net sales by 1%, adjusted operating income by 1%, and adjusted earnings per share by 1%.

During the fourth quarter, Consumer segment sales rose 4% to $1.13B and included a 1% currency benefit. Flavor Solutions segment sales were $723M, up 2%, and saw a 1% impact from forex rates.

Operating income was $311M in Q4 vs. $306M a year ago. Excluding special charges, adjusted operating income was $317M compared to $308M. In constant currency, adjusted operating income increased 3% from the year-ago period and included a 1% impact from currency, the company said.

Net income for the three months ended November 30 was $226.6M, or $0.84 per share, compared to $215.2M, or $0.80 per share, for the same period last year.

On an adjusted per-share basis, the company earned 86 cents, missing the average analyst estimate by two cents.

Net sales rose 3% to $1.85B and were in line with estimates.

data.title: WhiteFiber prices $210M convertible notes offering
data.description:
WhiteFiber (WYFI) said it has priced a $210M private offering of 4.5% convertible senior notes due 2031, upsized from the previously planned $200M.

The notes will pay 4.5% interest, mature on February 1, 2031, and can be converted into shares at an initial price of about $25.91 per share, a 27.5% premium to the stock’s recent price on January 21, 2026. The company also granted buyers an option to purchase up to an additional $20M of notes.

The sale of the notes is expected to close on January 26, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

The company estimates that the net proceeds from the offering will be around $202.1M, or ~$221.5M if the initial purchasers exercise their option to purchase additional notes in full. It plans to use around $120M of the proceeds to pay the cost of the zero-strike call option transaction and the remaining proceeds primarily for data center expansion.

WYFI --2.6% premarket to $19.79

Source: Press Release

data.title: Mobileye falls after 2026 revenue outlook seen below estimates
data.description:
Shares of Mobileye Global (MBLY) fell about 6% premarket on Thursday after the company's full year 2026 revenue guidance came below analysts' expectations.

Outlook

The Israeli company — which develops autonomous driving technology and advanced driver assistance systems — expects full year 2026 revenue to be between $1.9B and $1.98B (midpoint at $1.94B) versus a consensus revenue estimate of $2B.

The company said full-year 2026 financial guidance implies flat to 5% year-over-year revenue growth, including about 19% year-over-year revenue growth expected in the first quarter of 2026.

Mobileye said it expects 2026 adjusted operating income in the range of $170M to $220M.

Q4 Metrics

The company's fourth quarter 2025 revenue declined 9% year over year to $446M but surpassed analysts expectations. Meanwhile, adjusted EPS of $0.06 fell 58% year over year but was in-line with estimates.

Mobileye said the revenue declined mainly due to an 11% reduction in EyeQ SoC volumes. This was primarily related to a balancing of supply and demand that resulted in tighter-than-normal inventory levels at its Tier 1 customers exiting 2025, according to the company.

"I am proud of the Mobileye team for delivering 2025 results that were well above our initial expectations. We enter 2026 with strong momentum and a cash-generative business that enables us to fund continued investment in advanced product execution across our portfolio," said Mobileye's President and CEO Amnon Shashua.

Intel (INTC) owns a stake in Mobileye.

data.title: Dollar General expands its same-day delivery service
data.description:
Dollar General (DG) announced on Thursday an expansion of its same-day delivery service, myDG Delivery, which is now available through the company’s mobile app and website in more than 17,000 stores nationwide. The retailer said the service aims to provide greater convenience by allowing customers to order everyday items for home delivery, including in rural areas where on-demand options are often limited.

"We are proud to offer a service that not only brings convenience to our existing customers but also introduces new customers to the Dollar General brand through a frictionless digital experience," highlighted Dollar General (DG) vice president of digital commerce Lydia Thacher.

Looking ahead, the myDG Delivery service is intended to complement Dollar General's (DG) physical store footprint, with about 75% of the U.S. population living within five miles of a Dollar General location. The company continues to partner with major delivery platforms, including DoorDash (DASH), which services over 18,000 of its stores, and Uber Eats (UBER), now active at more than 17,000 locations. To promote the service, Dollar General (DH) will offer customers one free myDG Delivery order between January 20 and February 28, 2026, when using a myDG account. 

The Tennessee-based company noted that the expansion of myDG Delivery is part of its broader digital strategy, which includes the DG Media Network, its in-house retail media platform. With more than 7 million monthly active users across its digital channels and roughly 90 million addressable customer profiles, Dollar General (DG) said it is focused on increasing engagement and offering more personalized experiences for shoppers and brand partners.

Shares of Dollar General (DG) are up 10.8% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: OFG Bancorp GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.12, revenue of $185.4M beats by $1.23M
data.description:
• OFG Bancorp press release (OFG): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $185.4M (+1.9% Y/Y) beats by $1.23M.
  • Net interest margin of 5.12%, return on average assets of 1.81%, return on average tangible common stockholders’ equity of 17.20%, and efficiency ratio of 56.65%.
  • Total Provision for Credit Losses of $31.9 million compared to $28.3 million in 3Q25 and $30.2 million in 4Q24. 4Q25 primarily reflected $21.8 million for increased loan volume, $5.1 million for a specific reserve on a Puerto Rico telecommunications commercial loan, $2.4 million related to U.S. macroeconomic factors, and $1.7 million in charge-offs from the non-performing loans sale.
